verdict.note: YATU worktree backend :8911 + vite :5251, headless chromium. On un-ok'd latest (session-console/nav-mode-modifier-combos): '/' at composer line start opens the menu — /ok [ui] + /refuse [review], session-box grammar (shared SlashMenu). '/o' filters to /ok; Esc closes; 'see /ok' mid-prose opens nothing. On an ok'd reading (id-url-safe, header shows human-ok) the menu offers only /refuse — /ok gated exactly like the button.

YATU: on fresh un-ok'd dashboard-shell/board-unreachable-shows-retry, typed /ok + ⏎ → header flips to settled ☑ human-ok (button gone), sidecar gained one kind:human-ok event bound to the reading's ts (okTs 2026-07-12T07:36:07.504Z, by:human — same write as the button), feed default-hid the row (54→53) and the ☑ ok'd chip revealed it. The measurement's sidecar append was then reverted (git checkout) — agent-driven run, not a real human sign-off; the video is the proof.

YATU: /refuse + ⏎ prefilled the composer with the template, {node}/{scenario}/{expected} filled from the viewed reading (session-console/nav-mode-modifier-combos). Send → ONE open remark in the rail via the ordinary /api/remarks write; scenario staleness then names 'code · remark'. With a ⏱-stamped anchor first, /refuse kept the ▶0:00 line + its riding frame above the template. Remark landed in a disposable SPEXCODE_ISSUES_DIR store (linked-worktree backend refuses trunk commits) — no durable thread pollution.

'/' at line start opens the console-grammar menu ABOVE the composer container: /ok [ui] + /refuse [review]; '/o' filters to /ok; Esc closes keeping the draft; mid-prose '/' opens nothing; on an A/B scenario /ok is offered at the LATEST pole and NOT at the older pole (one registry gate); no header ok button exists — header carries only A/B nav + originator
Real Chromium: visible slash button preserved a non-empty draft and focus, opened the same /ok and /refuse menu as typing slash, filtered to /ok, and fit at 780px.
